title: ScenarioConfig
description: Complete input contract for one optimize-lab simulation scenario. A customer engagement = one file conforming to this schema. Version: 1.0.0

scenario_name.description: Human label, e.g. 'State University One-Stop — Fall Surge'

customer.description: Customer name for branded reports; omit for presets

location.description: Single location (v1). Operating hours bound the simulated day.

last_join_minutes_before_close.description: Door closes to new joins this many minutes before close

services.description: Service catalog. No service groups in v1.

target_duration_min.description: Org-declared target duration. Efficiency is measured against this.

demand_share.description: Fraction of total demand requesting this service; shares must sum to 1

appointment_eligible.description: If false, service is drop-in only

incomplete_rate.description: OPTIONAL. Historical fraction of started services marked incomplete (ID/docs/wrong service/fee refusal). Consumes employee time; prep_in_queue reduces it.

profile.description: Services this employee can deliver, with per-service performance. THIS MATRIX IS THE MODEL'S CORE NET-NEW DATA.

efficiency.description: target_duration / employee's actual avg duration. 2.0 = twice as fast as target; 0.8 = 20% slower. Effective duration = target / (efficiency × daily form).

csat.description: Employee's historical CSAT for this service (0–100). Source: VE.12.01 once live; estimate until then.

break_window.description: Default daily break. Staggered across staff; treated as a HARD constraint (no summoning during break, even in surge).

visitors_per_day_range.description: Optional day-to-day variation for Monte Carlo; if present, each simulated day draws uniformly from [low, high] and visitors_per_day is the nominal/reporting value.

surges.description: For single/double surge: time windows where arrival rate is multiplied.

custom_hourly_weights.description: For shape=custom: one relative weight per operating hour

language_preferences.description: Share of visitors requiring a specific language match.

policy.description: The operating rules being compared. 'baseline' describes the customer today; 'optimized' describes the lever stack to test.

appointment_share.description: Fraction of demand arriving as appointments TODAY. 0 = pure drop-in shop. Default mixed shop: 0.20.

scheduling_method.description: How appointments pair with employees. capacity/round_robin = pairing at summon; employee_specific = pinned at booking.

appointment_punctuality.description: Appointment punctuality promise (replaces the former symmetric grace window). All three inputs are customer-configurable.

early_summon_max_min.description: An appointment may be summoned up to this many minutes before its slot, and only once the visitor has checked in.

late_ok_min.description: Promise kept: lateness (summon time minus scheduled time, floored at 0) within this is on-time. Also bounds the collision-rule exception: an overrun drop-in is permitted only if the blocked appointment still starts within this.

late_acceptable_min.description: Tolerated on bad days, never the norm. Beyond this the CSAT lateness penalty grows convexly and the punctuality guardrail counts the start as unacceptable.

appointment_distribution.description: How booked appointment times spread across the day

optimized.description: Optimization levers in expected-impact order: matching, appointment_smoothing, prep_in_queue, deflection, running_late, break_scheduling. Each independently toggleable; results report solo impact per lever, combined impact of the selected set, and attribution. Routing weights are ADVANCED settings.

aging_cap_min.description: Hard fairness guard: any visitor waiting beyond this is summoned next regardless of score

weights.description: Routing score weights; must sum to 1. Advanced setting — presets preferred.

weight_preset.description: wait_dominant=0.6/0.4/0.0; balanced=0.34/0.33/0.33; fairness_first=0.0/0.9/0.1. Explicit weights override the preset.

target_appointment_share.description: Lever: shift demand from walk-in surge into evenly spread appointments up to this share

duration_reduction.description: ASSUMPTION FLAG: fraction of service duration removed by pre-arrival intake. Validate against real visit anatomy per customer.

incomplete_reduction.description: ASSUMPTION FLAG: fraction of would-be incompletes caught by pre-arrival prep (default 0.5 = midpoint of 40-60%).

rate.description: ASSUMPTION FLAG: fraction of visits resolved before arrival (self-service). Removed from demand; reported separately as 'resolved digitally'. Conservative default lowered 0.10 -> 0.02 by decision June 11, 2026; validate per customer before quoting externally.

no_show_reduction.description: Maps to VE.11.01. Fraction of would-be no-shows converted to served visits.

enabled.description: OPTIONAL lever: recommend a break stagger pattern fitted to the day's demand shape; reports impact like any other lever.

no_show_rate.description: Baseline appointment no-show rate; applies to both policies unless running_late modifies it

daily_form_jitter.description: Std-dev of per-day multiplier on each employee×service efficiency (today vs average)

random_seed.description: Fixed seed = reproducible demos and golden vectors

csat_model.description: Predicted-CSAT formula parameters: base × W_wait × W_accuracy × W_duration. As of v1.4 the walk-in promise is a RANGE around the dispatch-forward quote and W_accuracy is asymmetric (beta_late / beta_early).

beta_accuracy.description: Superseded in v1.4 by beta_late; retained as its fallback when beta_late is absent.

beta_late.description: Late-side promise-accuracy strength: penalty on (wait - promised_high)/center when service starts later than the promised range.

beta_early.description: ASSUMPTION FLAG: mild early-side strength. Deliberately low because the product's re-forecast updates (VE.02.01.US.09 pre-summon notification), >x% change alerts, range communication, and visitor-initiated push-back when summoned early absorb most over-delivery surprise. Fit from VE.12.01 ratings once live.

range_k.description: ASSUMPTION FLAG: promised-wait range half-width fraction; band = max(2 min, range_k x quote center). Fit from VE.12.01 + forecast logs once live.

wait_free_min.description: ASSUMPTION FLAG (v1.5 W_time): waits at or below this feel free - no absolute-time penalty. Walk-ins only. Fit from VE.12.01 ratings once live.

wait_ref_min.description: ASSUMPTION FLAG (v1.5 W_time): scaling reference for the absolute-wait disutility slope.

delta_wait.description: ASSUMPTION FLAG (v1.5 W_time): absolute-wait disutility strength - an accurately-promised wait of wait_ref_min costs ~delta_wait x (wait_ref - wait_free)/wait_ref of the score (about 21% at defaults). Expectation management softens waiting's cost; it does not erase time actually consumed.

time_floor.description: ASSUMPTION FLAG (v1.5 W_time): floor of the absolute-wait factor (reached at ~130 min at defaults).

abandonment_model.description: Abandonment computed as a function of predicted wait (throughput-only effect). Conservative v1 curve.

curve.description: ~2% at <=15 min rising to a 12-15% ceiling at 90+ min
